  "findings": [
    "Heat pump reversing valve (O/B terminal) is misconfigured in the thermostat settings, causing the system to heat when calling for cooling."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Access the installer setup menu on the thermostat and change the reversing valve energizing configuration (toggle from O to B, or B to O, depending on the manufacturer's default state)."
  ],
  "rationale": "The system is operating in reverse of the thermostat call because the reversing valve is energized on the wrong cycle (heating instead of cooling, or vice versa). This is a configuration failure rather than a physical component failure, but it prevents the system from functioning correctly.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer installation and operation manuals (thermostat configuration section)"
  ]
}
